answer:
**Iqbal's poem "Insaan" (Human Being), the 14th chapter of *Bang-e-Dara Part 2*, portrays humanity as a profound enigma and tragic figure in the grand design of creation.**[^1]

The poem opens by lamenting nature's (Qudrat's) "strange tyranny": God or creation fashions humanity as a secret (*raaz*) but conceals it from human sight itself.[^2] Humans burn with an unquenchable "thirst for knowing" (*zauq-e-aagahi*), yet life's deepest mystery (*bhed zindagi ka*) remains forever veiled.[^3] Existence feels like a disorienting hall of mirrors (*aaine ke ghar*), where origins and endings dissolve into wonder (*hairat aaghaaz-o-inteha*).[^4]

In stark contrast, the rest of creation revels in harmony and joy:
- The river's wave surges eagerly toward the sea.[^5]
- Wind bears clouds aloft on its shoulders.[^6]
- Stars revel drunkenly in their fated orbits, chained yet content in heaven's prison.[^7]
- The sun, a pious herald of dawn, later retreats westward to sip twilight's cup.[^8]

**Every created thing (*har shai*) delights in its own manifestation (*lazzat geer-e-wujood*), intoxicated by its visible form (*sar mast-e-mai-e-namood*). Humans alone lack a consoler for their grief (*gham-gusaar insaan*); their livelihood (*rozgaar*) is bitterly harsh (*talkh*).**[^9]

Iqbal thus depicts the human condition as uniquely alienated—restless, isolated in sorrow, and denied the instinctive fulfillment that nature otherwise bestows.

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 14 "Insaan" ([translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/)).
[^2]: Ibid., [p-4ea800](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-4ea800) and [p-d7ec8e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-d7ec8e).
[^3]: Ibid., [p-3a62d8](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-3a62d8).
[^4]: Ibid., [p-ed0bbf](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-ed0bbf).
[^5]: Ibid., [p-ad335e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-ad335e).
[^6]: Ibid., [p-5c7e5c](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-5c7e5c).
[^7]: Ibid., [p-3ebd11](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-3ebd11).
[^8]: Ibid., [p-394f46](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-394f46), [p-edc3e3](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-edc3e3).
[^9]: Ibid., [p-18d11b](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-18d11b) and [p-62d764](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-62d764).
